explanation: This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (bool_exp2), the next state is MANEUVER (bool_exp3) if a failure occurs (apfail). The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to check only the next state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units if a failure occurs (apfail).  The use of G implies that this should hold globally across all time steps. The implication ensures that the condition about the transition only has to be met when the system is in the standby state.

This LTL property states that when the autopilot is in STANDBY (bool_exp2), if there is a failure (apfail), then the autopilot will eventually transition to MANEUVER (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14. The use of F ensures the state change occurs, and the implication from standby to the eventual maneuver state ensures it only occurs in that circumstance and only if there is a failure.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, the next state will be MANEUVER if a failure occurs.  This captures the intent of the natural language requirement by using the atomic propositions to represent states and the condition triggering a state change.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, then in the next state it will transition to MANEUVER state upon the occurrence of a failure (apfail). It uses template ID 11 which is suitable for expressing a response to an event (apfail) causing a state transition. The boolean expressions are crafted to check the current state and the transition condition.

The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1, indicating that the transition should happen immediately or within the next time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, then in the next time step it will transition to MANEUVER state if apfail is true. This captures the requirement that the autopilot shall change states from STANDBY to MANEUVER when a failure occurs.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, if a failure (apfail) occurs, then the next state will be MANEUVER.  The index 11 is selected because it directly expresses the implication between the current state (bool_exp2) and the next state (bool_exp3) . We use a next operator to capture the transition to the MANEUVER state, which occurs after the failure.

It uses the implication operator to capture the causality between the failure and the state change. The G operator ensures that this property holds globally across all time steps. The F operator ensures the state change happens eventually.

The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode (state_is_MANEUVER).

The G operator ensures that this is always true. The -> operator indicates the implication from the condition to the consequence.  The F operator indicates that eventually the MANEUVER state will be reached after the failure has been detected. The N_DURATION allows for a limited time to make this transition.

It uses template 11 because we care about the next state given the current state and a failure condition. The N_DURATION value is not relevant to this property.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, if a failure (apfail) occurs, then it will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state within N_DURATION time units. The formula uses the implies operator (->) to express the conditional behavior. It also uses the Globally operator (G) to state that this implication must always hold true, and the Finally operator (F) to express that the autopilot will eventually be in MANEUVER.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, and a failure occurs (apfail), then in the next state the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state.  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1, indicating the next state transition will be considered. The property uses the atomic propositions state_is_STANDBY and state_is_MANEUVER from the input to represent the states of the autopilot.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and a failure occurs (apfail), the autopilot will eventually transition to MANEUVER mode within N_DURATION time units. The atomic proposition state_is_STANDBY checks if the system is in the standby state, apfail indicates a failure and state_is_MANEUVER checks the maneuver state. The implication (->) expresses the conditional behavior, and F[0, N_DURATION] indicates that the maneuver state will be reached within the specified time frame.

N_DURATION is set to 1 to reflect the immediate transition. The property also states that whenever the autopilot is in standby, it is not in maneuver mode. This is expressed by bool_exp1 and ensures the property only triggers upon entering standby.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and a failure occurs (apfail), then eventually within the next N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state.  The use of G ensures that this holds for all states. The use of -> ensures that only the specified state transitions can happen. The use of F[0, N_DURATION] implies that the autopilot transitions into the MANEUVER state in a bounded amount of time (N_DURATION).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, then it will transition to MANEUVER state in the next time step if a failure occurs.  The formula uses the atomic propositions state_is_STANDBY (true when the autopilot is in STANDBY), and apfail (true when a failure occurs). It leverages the next operator X to assert that the transition to MANEUVER state will happen in the next step. It uses implies operator -> to express the conditional transition. The N_DURATION is set to 1, which is consistent with the requirement for the immediate transition.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state (bool_exp3) within a duration of N_DURATION time units if there is a failure (apfail). The index 14 refers to the template G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)), which represents a globally true implication.  The implication states that if the condition bool_exp2 is true, then the condition bool_exp3 must also be true at some point within the given time window. This effectively captures the requirement that the autopilot should change its state from STANDBY to MANEUVER upon the occurrence of a failure.

The formula does not consider the autopilot's internal state transitions during the maneuver, but it does ensure the eventual change to the MANEUVER state when a failure is detected and the system is in STANDBY.
